findings.problem: Two contract acceptance checks have no test coverage: (1) CRLF-terminated snapshots must compare identically to LF-terminated snapshots — the stripping logic exists (src/sysdiff.c:248-253) and appears correct but is unverified by any fixture; (2) a snapshot line exceeding SYSDIFF_MAX_LINE_BYTES and a snapshot exceeding SYSDIFF_MAX_SNAPSHOT_ENTRIES must each return exit 2 with empty stdout and a contextual diagnostic — no test exercises either limit path.
findings.proposed_fix: Add test_crlf_snapshots_compare_identically_to_lf that writes the same logical snapshot content with \r\n and \n endings and asserts identical diff output. Add test_line_too_long_fails and test_too_many_entries_fails that generate inputs at the boundary and assert exit 2, empty stdout, and a stderr fragment naming the exceeded limit.

findings.problem: CRLF-terminated lines can have at most SYSDIFF_MAX_LINE_BYTES - 1 data bytes while LF-terminated lines can have exactly SYSDIFF_MAX_LINE_BYTES. The check `ch != '\n' && len >= SYSDIFF_MAX_LINE_BYTES` exempts '\n' from the limit guard but not '\r'. A CRLF line with N data bytes has len == N when '\r' is read; at N == SYSDIFF_MAX_LINE_BYTES the guard triggers LINE_TOO_LONG. The diagnostic prints 'maximum 65536 bytes' but the effective maximum for CRLF files is 65535 data bytes.
findings.proposed_fix: Extend the guard to also exempt '\r' when a following '\n' is expected, or document the 1-byte discrepancy in the limit comment and the README.

findings.problem: The valgrind-test target depends on build/sysdiff but does not rebuild it. If make sanitizer-test has already run (which performs make clean and rebuilds with -fsanitize=address,undefined), the binary left in build/ is an ASan-instrumented binary. Running make valgrind-test directly after make sanitizer-test causes valgrind to abort with a shadow-memory range conflict. The make-quality aggregate target avoids this by inserting a clean GCC rebuild between sanitizer-test and valgrind-test, but standalone invocation of make valgrind-test is unreliable.
findings.proposed_fix: Add an explicit prerequisite that rebuilds build/sysdiff without sanitizers before valgrind runs, or document clearly that valgrind-test must only be invoked via make-quality or after a fresh clean non-sanitized build.

checks_run.summary: All Python source files byte-compiled with no syntax errors.
GCC strict build passed with -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Werror -O2; zero warnings.
Clang strict build passed with same flags; zero warnings.
Shell fixture (test_sysdiff.sh -> test_sysdiff_fixture.sh) passed all scenarios.
17 tests collected and passed; fixture compiles from source with strict flags.
ASan+UBSan build with clang ran shell fixture clean; no memory or UB errors.

checks_run.command: make clean && make CC=gcc && make valgrind-test
checks_run.summary: Valgrind --leak-check=full reported no memory errors against clean non-sanitized binary.

correctness.justification: All documented behaviors verified: exit codes 0/1/2, output formats for added/removed/changed/no-change, sorted merge walk, partial-output prevention on both before and after parse errors, empty-value round-trip, comment and blank-line skipping, duplicate-key detection, embedded-NUL rejection. No correctness bugs found.

security.justification: All fprintf/fputs format strings are compile-time literals. fopen uses binary mode. Embedded NUL bytes are detected before strchr or strcmp. file.* keys are treated as opaque data and never opened as filesystem paths. copy_range guards len+1 overflow before malloc. Line buffer growth guards overflow at cap*2 and SIZE_MAX/sizeof(char).

memory-ownership.justification: goto cleanup pattern makes ownership explicit: parse_snapshot owns snapshot resources until it returns 0. snapshot_free zeroes fields making double-calls safe. All allocation paths pair correctly with free. read_line frees its buffer on every error return. fclose failure path sets file=NULL before goto cleanup to prevent double-close. No leaks or double-frees found.

resource-limits.justification: SYSDIFF_MAX_LINE_BYTES and SYSDIFF_MAX_SNAPSHOT_ENTRIES are defined and enforced with contextual diagnostics. The Low finding is a 1-byte discrepancy between CRLF and LF lines at the boundary of the line limit.

test-adequacy.justification: 17 pytest cases cover all key-syntax rejection cases, output format variants, input-order determinism, error isolation, directory/missing-file errors, and embedded NUL rejection. Missing: CRLF equivalence fixture, line-length limit rejection test, entry-count limit rejection test — all explicitly required by contract acceptance checks.

build-and-toolchain.justification: GCC and Clang strict builds pass. Sanitizer coverage is available via make sanitizer-test with graceful skip when clang is absent. Valgrind coverage is available via make-quality or a manual clean build sequence. Standalone make valgrind-test after make sanitizer-test is broken due to the ASan binary being left in build/.
